planContent:
# 🏗️ Architecture Blueprint

## App Overview
A client-side PDF document manipulation tool that allows users to upload a PDF, visualize its pages as image thumbnails, select specific ranges or individual pages for extraction, and download the resulting sub-document without ever uploading data to a server.

## Core Features
- Local File Upload: File input restricted to `.pdf` format.
- Page Previews: Render PDF pages as high-fidelity thumbnails using PDF.js.
- Page Selection: Click-to-toggle selection for individual pages.
- Range Extraction: Input field for specific page ranges (e.g., "1-3, 5").
- Bulk Actions: Select All, Deselect All, and Invert Selection buttons.
- PDF Generation: Re-construct a new PDF document from selected pages.
- Dark Mode Interface: Full dark-themed UI with high-contrast accessibility.

## UI & Layout Strategy
- **Layout type**: Single-column vertical stack with a fixed header and responsive grid for thumbnails.
- **Header**: Contains App Title ("FORGE PDF Splitter"), Upload File button (custom-styled), and a "Download Selection" button (disabled until selection exists).
- **Main content area**: A scrollable canvas area displaying a grid of thumbnail cards. Each card contains a thumbnail image, page number label, and a selection checkbox.
- **Controls**: Sticky control bar below the header containing Page Range Input, Select All/None buttons, and current page count indicators.
- **Footer**: "FORGE | Local-First PDF Utility | No data leaves your machine"
- **Color scheme**: FORGE dark theme (--bg:#0a0a0a, --surface:#171717, --accent:#3b82f6, --text:#e5e5e5, --border:#262626).
- **Typography**: Inter (sans-serif).
- **Responsive strategy**: Thumbnails grid switches from 4-col (desktop) to 2-col (tablet) to 1-col (mobile).
- **Animations**: Subtle scale-up transition on card hover and fade-in for thumbnails once rendered.

## Data Model & State
| Variable | Type | Default | Description |
|----------|------|---------|-------------|
| pdfDoc | Object | null | The loaded PDFDocumentProxy object. |
| pages | Array | [] | Array of objects: { pageNumber: number, dataUrl: string, selected: boolean }. |
| fileName | String | "" | Name of the original uploaded file. |
| isProcessing | Boolean | false | Flag to disable UI interactions during heavy rendering. |

- **localStorage key**: N/A (State is ephemeral for security and privacy; session-based only).
- **Persistence strategy**: None; pages are cleared on refresh to ensure privacy.

## Technical Stack & Libraries
- **Required CDNs**: 
    - https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/pdf.js/3.11.174/pdf.min.js
    - https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/pdf-lib/1.17.1/pdf-lib.min.js
- **Allowed**: PDF.js (rendering), PDF-lib (manipulation)
- **APIs**: FileSystem Access API (for triggerring download)

## Implementation Steps
1. Setup document skeleton with FORGE dark CSS variables.
2. Initialize PDF.js worker via CDN.
3. Implement file input listener to capture `File` object.
4. Use PDF.js to load the document and loop through pages.
5. Convert each page to a data URL (canvas rendering) to display in the thumbnail grid.
6. Create an state array `pages` tracking `pageNumber` and `selected` boolean.
7. Implement event listener for thumbnail clicks to toggle `selected` state and CSS border styling.
8. Build a parser for the "Range Input" string that updates the `selected` boolean in the `pages` array.
9. Implement "Select All/None/Invert" functions to update DOM classes and state.
10. Integrate `pdf-lib` to copy selected pages into a new `PDFDocument`.
11. Implement trigger to download the generated Uint8Array as a blob.
12. Add global loading spinner logic for `isProcessing` state.

## Edge Cases & Error Handling
- **Invalid PDF**: Catch Promise rejection from `pdfjsLib.getDocument()` and display a toast alert "Invalid PDF file".
- **Empty State**: Show a prominent "Drop PDF here" overlay when no file is loaded.
- **Large Files**: Implement `isProcessing` flag to prevent concurrent render calls.
- **Selection Logic**: Ensure ranges like "1-10" are validated against actual total page count; truncate if input exceeds page count.
- **Memory**: Clear individual `dataUrl` strings from memory if needed for extremely large PDF uploads.
